title: Send data to stdout, diagnostics to stderr
description: Write only the program's actual output to stdout and all logs, progress, and errors to stderr so piping stays clean.
body: Use when a CLI produces output a script might consume. Do not print progress bars, warnings, or errors to stdout, and do not interleave logs with data. Actions: route the result payload (the thing a pipe should receive) to stdout, and send everything else (status, warnings, errors, prompts) to stderr; verify with `tool > out.txt` that out.txt holds only data. Validation signals include redirecting stdout to a file and finding no diagnostic noise, and a downstream pipe parsing cleanly. Risk boundary: leaking diagnostics into stdout silently corrupts the input of the next command in a pipeline — treat it as a correctness bug, not cosmetics.
